volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Casina (Section 2.7.435 - Section 3.1.525b)

sections.label: Section 2.7.435
sections.translation: My well-wishers and friends [*] are coming out . Here in ambush I ’ ll lay in wait against them .

sections.label: Section 2.8.437
sections.original: Sine modo rus veniat : ego remittam ad te virum
sections.translation: Only let him come into the country ; I ’ ll send the fellow back into the city to you with his porter ’ s knot [*] , as black as a collier .

sections.label: Section 2.8.439
sections.translation: So it ought to be .

sections.label: Section 2.8.439b
sections.translation: I ’ ll have that done and well taken care of .

sections.label: Section 2.8.440
sections.original: Volui Chalinum , si domi esset , mittere
sections.translation: I intended , if he had been at home , to send Chalinus to cater with you ; that , even in his sadness , I might , in addition , inflict this misfortune upon our foe .

sections.label: Section 2.8.443
sections.original: Recessim cedam ad parietem , imitabor nepam ;
sections.translation: I ’ ll betake me back again to the wall ; I ’ ll imitate the crab . Their conversation must be secretly picked up by me ;

sections.label: Section 2.8.445
sections.original: nam illorum me alter cruciat , alter macerat .
sections.translation: for the one of them is tormenting me , the other wasting me with anguish . Why , this whip-rascal is marching along in his white garb [*] , a very receptacle for stripes . My own death I defer ; I ’ m determined to send this fellow to Acheron before me .

sections.label: Section 2.8.449
sections.original: Vt tibi ego inventus sum obsequens . quod maxime
sections.translation: How obsequious have I been found to you ! A thing that you especially

sections.label: Section 2.8.450
sections.translation: desired , that same have I put in your power ; this day the object that you love shall be with you , unknown to your wife .

sections.label: Section 2.8.451b
sections.translation: Hush ! So may the Deities kindly bless me , I can hardly withhold my lips from kissing you on account of this , my own delight !

sections.label: Section 2.8.454
sections.original: Quid , deosculere ? quae res ? quae voluptas tua ?
sections.translation: What ? Kiss him ? What ’ s the meaning of this ? What ’ s this delight of yours ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.455
sections.original: ecfodere hercle hic volt , credo , vesicam vilico .†
sections.translation: I really do think he intends to choke the bailiff .

sections.label: Section 2.8.456
sections.translation: Do you love me at all now ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.456b
sections.original: Immo edepol me quam te minus .
sections.translation: Aye , by my faith , myself even less than you . May I embrace you ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.457c
sections.translation: You may .

sections.label: Section 2.8.458
sections.original: Vt , quia te tango , mel mihi videor lingere .
sections.translation: How , when I touch you , I do seem to myself to be tasting honey !

sections.label: Section 2.8.459
sections.original: Vltro te , amator , apage te a dorso meo .
sections.translation: Away with you , you lover ; get off , with your too close acquaintanceship !

sections.label: Section 2.8.460
sections.original: Illuc est , illuc , quod hic hunc fecit vilicum :
sections.translation: This is the reason , this is it why he made him his bailiff ; some time ago , too , when I came in his way , he wanted to make me his chamberlain upon the like terms .

sections.label: Section 2.8.463
sections.original: Vt tibi morigerus hodie , ut voluptati fui .
sections.translation: How subservient have I proved to you to-day , how attentive to your pleasure !

sections.label: Section 2.8.464
sections.original: Vt tibi , dum vivam , bene velim plus quam mihi .
sections.translation: How surely , so long as I live , will I prove more of a well-wisher to you than to my own self !

sections.label: Section 2.8.465
sections.original: Hodie hercle , opinor , hi conturbabunt pedes :
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I think that [*] this very day they ’ ll be making terms . Surely , this old fellow is an universal admirer .

sections.label: Section 2.8.467
sections.original: Vt ego hodie Casinam deosculabor , ut mihi
sections.translation: How will I this day give full many a kiss to Casina ! How will I , unknown to my wife , right pleasantly enjoy myself !

sections.label: Section 2.8.468b
sections.translation: Oho ! Now , faith , at last I ’ ve got into the right track .

sections.label: Section 2.8.470
sections.original: hic ipsus Casinam deperit . habeo viros .
sections.translation: It ’ s himself that ’ s dying for Casina . I ’ ve caught the fellows .

sections.label: Section 2.8.471
sections.original: Iam hercle amplexari , iam osculari gestio .
sections.translation: Even now , by my troth , am I longing to embrace her ; even now to be kissing her .

sections.label: Section 2.8.472
sections.original: Sine prius deduci . quid , malum , properas ?
sections.translation: Do let her be brought out first from the house . Why the plague are you in such a hurry ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.472b
sections.translation: I ’ m in love .

sections.label: Section 2.8.473
sections.original: At non opinor fieri hoc posse hodie .
sections.translation: But I don ’ t think that this can possibly be managed to-day .

sections.label: Section 2.8.473b
sections.translation: It can , if , indeed , you think that you can possibly receive your freedom to-morrow .

sections.label: Section 2.8.475
sections.original: Enim véro huc aures magis sunt adhibendae mihi :
sections.translation: Why , really , I must make still better use here of my ears ; now , in one thicket , I shall be cleverly catching two boars .

sections.label: Section 2.8.477
sections.original: Apud hunc sodalem meum atque vicinum mihi
sections.translation: At the house of this friend and neighbour of mine there ’ s a place provided ; I have confided to him all my amorousness : he said that he would find me a room .

sections.label: Section 2.8.480
sections.translation: What will his wife do ? Where will she be ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.480b
sections.translation: I ’ ve cleverly contrived that : my wife will invite her here , to her own house , to the wedding ; to be here with her , to help her , to sleep with her . I have requested it , and my wife has said that she will do so . She ’ ll be sleeping here : I ’ ll take care her husband is away from home .

sections.label: Section 2.8.485
sections.original: tu rus uxorem duces : id rus hic erit
sections.translation: You shall take your wife home into the country ; that country shall be this house , for a period , until I ’ ve had my marriage with Casina . Hence , before daylight , you shall afterwards take her home to-morrow . Isn ’ t it very skilfully managed ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.488b
sections.translation: Cleverly !

sections.label: Section 2.8.488c
sections.translation: Only do proceed ; contrive away . By my troth , to your own mischance are you so clever .

sections.label: Section 2.8.490
sections.translation: Do you know what you must do now ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.490b
sections.translation: Tell me .

sections.label: Section 2.8.490c
sections.translation: Take this purse . Be off and buy some provisions : make haste . But I want it nicely done : delicate eatables , just as she herself is a delicate bit .

sections.label: Section 2.8.492b
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 2.8.493
sections.translation: Buy some cuttle-fish , mussels , calamaries , barley-fish [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.8.494b
sections.translation: Aye , wheaten fish , if you know what you ’ re about .

sections.label: Section 2.8.495
sections.translation: Some sole-fish [*] .

sections.label: Section 2.8.495b
sections.translation: Prithee , why those rather than soles of wood , with which your head may be banged , you most vile old fellow ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.497
sections.translation: Should you like some tongue-fish [*] ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.497b
sections.original: Quid opust , quando uxor domi est ?
sections.translation: What need is there , since my wife ’ s at home ? She is our tongue-fish , for she ’ s never silent .

sections.label: Section 2.8.499
sections.original: In re praesenti ex copia piscaria
sections.translation: While I ’ m about it , I must make choice out of the supply of fish what to purchase .

sections.label: Section 2.8.500b
sections.translation: You say what ’ s good : be off . I don ’ t care to spare for cost ; provide abundantly . But it ’ s requisite also that I should see this neighbour of mine , that he may attend to what I ’ ve requested .

sections.label: Section 2.8.503b
sections.translation: Am I to go now ?

sections.label: Section 2.8.503c
sections.translation: I wish you .

sections.label: Section 2.8.504
sections.translation: By three freedoms I could not be induced

sections.label: Section 2.8.505
sections.original: quin ego illis hodie comparem magnum malum
sections.translation: this day to do other than provide a heavy retribution for them , and at once disclose all this matter to my mistress . I ’ ve caught and fully detected my enemies in their guilt . But if my mistress is ready now to do her duty , the cause is all our own : I ’ ll cleverly be beforehand with the fellows .

sections.label: Section 2.8.510
sections.original: nostro omine it dies ; iam victi vicimus .
sections.translation: With omens in our favour the day proceeds : just conquered , we are the conquerors . I ’ ll go indoors , that that which another cook has seasoned , I now , in my turn , may season after another fashion ; and that for him for whom it was prepared , it may really not be prepared ; and that that may be prepared for him , which before was not prepared [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.515
sections.original: Núnc amici ánne inimici sís imago , Alcésime ,
sections.translation: Now , Alcesimus , I shall know whether you are the very picture of friend or foe to me ; now is the proof upon view ; now is the contest going on . But why do I do so ; forbear to correct me ; save yourself all that . With your hoary head , at an age unfit ; save yourself that as well . One who has a wife ; save yourself that like wise .

sections.label: Section 3.1.520
sections.original: Miseriorem ego ex amore quam te vidi neminem .
sections.translation: I never saw a person more distracted with love than yourself .

sections.label: Section 3.1.521
sections.translation: Do take care that the house is clear .

sections.label: Section 3.1.521b
sections.translation: Why , faith , men-servants , maid-servants , all of them I ’ m determined to send out of the house to yours .

sections.label: Section 3.1.522b
sections.translation: Heyday ! with your adroitness you are very adroit ! But only take care and remember the lines which Colax repeats [*] ; take care that every one comes with his own provisions , as if they were going to Sutrium [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.525
sections.translation: I ’ ll remember it .

sections.label: Section 3.1.525b
sections.original: Em , nunc enim te demum nullum scitum scitiust .
sections.translation: Why now there ’ s no public ordinance [*] better ordered than yourself , in fact . Attend to this . I ’ m now going to the Forum ; I shall be here just now .
